skill.name: tiktok-automation
skill.description: Guide AI agents through TikTok publishing, photo posting, content listing, and profile checks using the Rube MCP TikTok toolkit.
skill.summary: Automate TikTok publishing and account workflows through Rube MCP.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static external command, weak cryptography, and system reconnaissance detections are false positives caused by Markdown inline code and ordinary workflow text. The confirmed risk is networked use of Rube MCP and TikTok OAuth to publish or inspect account content, which can change a user's external social media account.

medium_findings.title: External TikTok MCP Workflow Can Modify Account Content
medium_findings.description: The skill directs users to connect to the Rube MCP endpoint and use TikTok OAuth, then run upload, photo post, publish, and account lookup workflows. This is legitimate for the skill purpose, but it can create public posts or access account data through an external service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The workflow explicitly depends on an external MCP server and authenticated TikTok tools. The intent is legitimate, but the account-changing side effects are clear from the documented tool sequences.

low_findings.title: External Command Detections Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The static analyzer treated Markdown backticks around tool names, parameters, and examples as Ruby or shell backtick execution. SKILL.md is documentation and contains no executable Ruby, shell script, or command interpolation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Every cited instance is inline Markdown or a fenced workflow example. No evidence found of executable code or a shell command path.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detections Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The flagged lines describe the skill and list video response metadata. They do not contain cryptographic code, hashing calls, encryption settings, or weak algorithm use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are natural language documentation. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or any other cryptographic implementation.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Detections Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The flagged lines discuss TikTok publish status polling, a workflow example, and a quick reference table. They do not instruct the agent to inspect the host system, enumerate files, or collect environment details.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is TikTok API status checking, not host reconnaissance. No evidence found of commands such as uname, whoami, env, ls, or network discovery.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded MCP URL Is a Documented Service Endpoint
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL points to the Rube MCP server that the skill requires for TikTok tool access. It is a real network dependency, but it is disclosed as setup guidance rather than hidden exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is visible in the setup section and matches the declared Rube MCP dependency. It still requires user trust in the external MCP provider.

content.value_statement: TikTok publishing often requires several upload, status, and publish steps. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured workflow for TikTok actions through Rube MCP.
actual_capabilities: Guides video upload and publish flows with TikTok tool sequencing.
Explains photo posting through the TikTok toolkit.
Shows how to poll publish status after uploads or posts.
Documents listing authenticated user videos with cursor pagination.
Describes profile, basic account, and account statistics lookups.
Lists key parameters, privacy levels, and common TikTok API pitfalls.
limitations: Requires Rube MCP and an active TikTok OAuth connection.
Does not publish without the external TikTok toolkit.
Only supports authenticated user data described by the TikTok tools.
TikTok rate limits, moderation, and account permissions still apply.

use_cases.title: Schedule a creator upload workflow
use_cases.description: Prepare the correct upload, status, and publish steps for a short-form video post.

use_cases.title: Build a social media operations checklist
use_cases.description: Standardize TikTok publishing parameters, privacy levels, and status checks for repeatable posting.

use_cases.title: Inspect account publishing results
use_cases.description: List published videos and review profile or statistics data for the authenticated account.

prompt_templates.title: Publish one video
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner workflow for a single video post.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the TikTok automation skill to publish one video. Confirm the Rube TikTok connection, upload the file, check status, and publish with SELF_ONLY privacy first.

prompt_templates.title: Post a photo update
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the TikTok automation skill to post a photo with a caption. Check the current tool schema first and explain each required field before posting.

prompt_templates.title: Review recent videos
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the TikTok automation skill to list my recent videos. Handle pagination and summarize titles, share links, durations, and publish times.

prompt_templates.title: Run a guarded launch workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced publish flow with safety checks.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the TikTok automation skill to run a guarded launch. Verify OAuth status, validate media requirements, upload, poll status with backoff, confirm privacy settings, then publish.

output_examples.input: Publish this product teaser video with comments disabled.
output: Connection status confirmed for the TikTok toolkit.
Video upload started and publish status is being checked.
Ready to publish with comments disabled after you confirm the final caption and privacy level.

output_examples.input: Show me my latest TikTok videos.
output: Recent videos were retrieved for the authenticated account.
The summary includes title, share link, duration, and creation time.
More results are available if you want the next cursor page.

output_examples.input: Check whether my upload finished processing.
output_examples.output: The publish status check returns the current processing state and any failure details. If processing continues, the workflow waits before checking again.

best_practices: Search Rube tools first so current TikTok schemas are used.
Confirm privacy, caption, and interaction settings before publishing.
Use reasonable polling intervals and backoff to avoid rate limits.
anti_patterns: Do not assume upload and publish are a single operation.
Do not publish publicly before reviewing the final media and caption.
Do not ignore OAuth scope errors or expired TikTok connections.

faq.question: Does this skill connect directly to TikTok?
faq.answer: It uses the Rube MCP TikTok toolkit, so Rube MCP and TikTok OAuth must be configured first.

faq.question: Can it publish videos automatically?
faq.answer: It can guide the upload and publish sequence when the required tools and permissions are available.

faq.question: Can it post photos?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documents the TikTok photo post tool and the required photo, caption, and privacy fields.

faq.question: Can it read other users' profiles?
faq.answer: No. The documented profile tools return data for the authenticated TikTok account only.

faq.question: Why does the workflow poll publish status?
faq.answer: TikTok processing can take time after upload. Polling confirms readiness or reports failures before the next step.

faq.question: What is the main security consideration?
faq.answer: The skill uses an external MCP service and authenticated TikTok account actions. Users should verify settings before publishing.
